title: The Platform — Three Phases
subtitle: Free app (acquisition) → premium (monetization) → smart hardware (upgrade layer, moat)
description: The spine of the company shown once, plainly. A free app widens the top of the funnel, a premium tier narrows but monetizes the middle, and the hardware layer deepens revenue at the bottom. The stage-to-stage conversion rates are deliberately empty: they are Phase 1 measurement questions, not assumptions we get to skip. With zero users today there is no measured activation, retention, or free-to-paid conversion to show.
SYNTHETIC_WARNING: The stage widths are illustrative of the land-and-expand shape only. Every conversion number is unmeasured and rendered empty on purpose. Do not read a funnel volume as a user count.

data.required_input: Organic/community-led acquisition rate and activation rate — awaiting Phase 1 launch
data.note: Widest stage by design: no price barrier, any smallholder with a phone and a pond qualifies.

data.required_input: Free-to-paid conversion rate — cannot exist before users do
data.note: Need-triggered upgrade once a farm outgrows manual logging. This is where AquaVision becomes a subscription-plus-take-rate business.

data.required_input: Share of premium farms that outgrow software-only tracking; dock/service capacity
data.note: A hardware sale into a customer the platform already knows — not a cold sale to a stranger. The data moat is built before the hardware ships.

rendering_guidance: Render the three stages with EMPTY value cells and the required-input labels filled. The honesty of the empty cells is the point — the shape communicates the strategy; the missing numbers communicate the discipline.
